title: chenopodium album subsp. pseudopulifolium
intro: Chenopodium album subsp. pseudopulifolium, also known as Lamb's Quarters or Wild Spinach, is a plant species that belongs to the Amaranthaceae family. It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and is valued for its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal properties. In addition, the leaves of Chenopodium album subsp. pseudopulifolium can be eaten raw or cooked, similar to spinach, providing a rich source of vitamins and minerals.
